era_context: Egypt's Old Kingdom, Dynasty IV. The pyramid-building age continues. Khufu's reign (if it extended this far) or his successor's reign. Mesopotamia was still in the Early Dynastic III period, before Akkadian dominance. The Indus Valley Civilisation was developing urban centres. This represents the apogee of Old Kingdom Egyptian state power.

events.title: Dynasty IV pharaonic rule; Giza pyramid complex in use or construction
events.description: This year falls within Dynasty IV of Egypt's Old Kingdom. The Giza pyramid complex was either in active construction or in early use as a royal mortuary complex. The pharaonic state maintained its centralised administrative apparatus, controlling vast labour forces, agricultural surplus, and trade networks. The construction and maintenance of pyramid complexes was the primary expression of pharaonic power and religious ideology.
events.certainty_note: Secure within Dynasty IV, but no year-specific events are documented. Chronological uncertainty of ±50–100 years.

disconfirming_evidence: No disconfirming evidence identified for events listed.
historiographic_note: The historical record is entirely archaeological. No written records survive. The bias toward monumental architecture means that the vast majority of the population's experiences are invisible. Chronological uncertainty is inherent to this period.
